commit.message:
Disable CUDA graph caching to fix deterministic bad_alloc at call 30

Root cause: ExLlamaV2's QAttn.graph_map caches CUDA graphs keyed by
(q_len, batch_size). Graphs hardcode tensor memory addresses at capture
time. Our sweep creates fresh tensor allocations each forward pass —
when a cached graph is replayed, it writes to stale addresses from the
original capture, corrupting memory until bad_alloc on ~30th call.

Fix: set config.no_graphs = True (checks EXLLAMA_NO_GRAPHS env var
internally). This disables CUDA graph capture/replay entirely, forcing
fresh kernel launches each call. Slightly slower but correct.

Also added call counter + cache.current_seq_len debug logging.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

refactor: progressive disclosure uses anchor slugs instead of line numbers

(e.g., #origin-story) are stable across revisions and work as markdown
links for both human readers and agents.

commit.message:
Fresh Params per module call to prevent attn_mask/tensor accumulation

Params lazily caches attn_mask, past_lens_tensor, block_diag_mask,
and moves position_offsets in-place. Sharing one Params across 48+
module calls per forward pass accumulated stale GPU tensor references
that caused std::bad_alloc in ext_c.q_attn_forward_1 after ~200
forward passes (~7th question of probe 2).

Fix: _fresh_params() factory creates a new Params for every module.forward()
call in forward_with_path, forward_with_hooks, and project_to_vocab.

dell-precision-5680: Fix webcam by overriding icamerasrc-ipu6ep HAL

nixpkgs 25.11 has a bug where gst_all_1.icamerasrc-ipu6ep resolves to
the Tiger Lake ipu6-camera-hal instead of ipu6ep-camera-hal (Raptor Lake).
This causes v4l2-relayd-ipu6 to fail loading ipu6ep.so in a crash loop.

Add a nixpkgs overlay to pass the correct HAL. Fixed upstream in
nixos-unstable; overlay can be removed after upgrading.

commit.message:
Test Theory 1: remove empty_cache() to fix CUDA fragmentation

Previous approach (fresh cache + gc + empty_cache per call) thrashed
the CUDA heap — hundreds of alloc/free cycles fragmented memory until
ext_c.q_attn_forward_1 couldn't find a contiguous block (std::bad_alloc).

Now: reuse single persistent cache with reset(), no empty_cache().
Let PyTorch's caching allocator handle memory reuse.

Added DEBUG mem stats logging (allocated GB + alloc_retries) to
diagnose whether fragmentation was the root cause.

  numbering (round-1/round-2/round-3 not session-0/session-1).

commit.message:
Fresh cache per generate_short with proper current_seq_len management

Previous attempts failed because:
- Fresh cache without current_seq_len management: cache never worked
- Persistent cache with reset(): C extension state accumulated over
  ~24 calls causing std::bad_alloc on next probe
- empty_cache() alone: didn't help (leak is in C extension, not PyTorch)

This version: del old cache + gc.collect + empty_cache + create new.
Only one cache exists at a time (no OOM). Fresh C extension state
each call. Proper current_seq_len management (0 → prompt_len → +1).

commit.message:
Fix duplicate gonio files and add biomech detection TODOs

Fix: preOA import_biomechanics assigned the same goniometer file to
both FE and STS maneuvers (38 recordings affected). Add maneuver-to-
file keyword matching (_match_file_to_maneuver) that:
- Matches filenames containing "flex"/"fe" to FE, "sit"/"stand" to STS
- Treats single ambiguous files as FE-only (STS returns empty)
- Falls back to first file with warning for multiple ambiguous files

Add TODO(biomech-detection) markers for sequential improvement work:
- Flick detection: cycling peaks mistaken for flicks (~10 candidates
  per recording, real flick barely distinguishable by score)
- Cycle onset: half-cycle offset from rolling-range backtrack
- Pause scoring: permissive threshold + confidence scoring for
  inter-cycle pause detection and cycle counting
- End detection: inconsistent (4/54 missing) due to post-cycling
  fidgeting and velocity-threshold sensitivity

Fix seed upsert: replace existing entries instead of skipping

seedHistory was skipping entries with matching IDs, so v2 seed data

fix: write config to ~/.config/cipher/ not pipx venv

cipher setup was writing config.yaml to REPO_ROOT which in pipx
installs points to the venv share dir. load_config() looks in
~/.config/cipher/ as the user config location. Now writes there
first, and also to project root if in dev mode.

Adjust WHO section to start at WHO logo/title heading

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: classify 429 as transient, bail on stale lease during secret failure handling

Vault 429 rate-limit classification:
- Added 429 to transient status codes alongside 5xx in
  classify_vault_error. Previously 429 fell into Other (permanent),
  causing tasks to fail instead of requeue on Vault rate limiting.
- New classify_api_429_as_unavailable test.

Stale lease safety:
- handle_transient_secret_failure now bails early if
  release_assignment fails (lease expired). Previously the error was
  logged but run state was still forced to Pending, creating divergent
  task/run state when the background sweep already owns cleanup.
- handle_permanent_secret_failure now bails early if tasks.complete
  returns None (stale lease). Same reasoning: the sweep handles
  terminal transitions for expired leases.
- New transient_failure_with_expired_lease_defers_to_sweep test that
  expires the lease during secret resolution and verifies the handler
  bails without requeuing.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add torch.cuda.empty_cache() per generate_short to fix fragmentation

After ~18K attention forward calls (24 questions × 48 layers × ~16
decode steps), CUDA memory fragments enough that q_attn_forward_1
can't allocate its scratch buffer → std::bad_alloc. Calling
empty_cache() at the start of each generate_short() reclaims freed
blocks back to the CUDA allocator.

Fix: return empty trades when all accounts are disconnected

When visibleAccountIds was empty (all accounts hidden), the .in() filter
was skipped and ALL trades were returned regardless. Now all three

- /api/client-performance
